message:
test(atc): rename + invert two file-backed ATC tests to assert the no-op contract while the durable-experience-store writable gate is held off

Two test cases for file-backed ATC behaviour
(`conflict_reservation_resolution_updates_file_backed_mailboxes`
and `capture_atc_execution_result_updates_file_backed_mailboxes`)
were asserting the post-write transitions that are intentionally
gated off right now while the ATC state migration to an isolated
canonical store is in flight.  `atc_durable_experience_store_writable(&pool)`
returns true only for `:memory:` pools during the migration, so
both functions short-circuit on a file-backed pool and leave
the durable rows untouched.

Both tests had been silently passing because the assertion they
made happened to coincide with what the no-op produced — but the
post-migration "real" behaviour will look exactly the same except
the experience rows DO transition.  When the migration lands and
the gate flips, these tests should re-assert the transition;
keeping them in their old "asserts the transition" form makes
that distinction invisible.

Renamed and inverted both:

  conflict_reservation_resolution_is_noop_on_file_backed_mailboxes
    Asserts `atc_durable_experience_store_writable(&pool) ==
    false` up front (so the test is self-documenting about why
    it asserts no-op behaviour), then asserts the open
    experience remains visible (`remaining.len() == 1` with
    `state == Executed`) instead of being transitioned to
    Resolved.  Documents the flip-back instructions in a
    comment block.

  capture_atc_execution_result_is_noop_on_file_backed_mailboxes
    Same pattern: writable-gate assertion up front, then
    asserts that the planned-but-not-transitioned experience
    stays in `Planned` and `fetch_open_atc_experiences`
    (which filters to executed/open) returns an empty result.
    Documents both the gate behaviour and the fetch filter
    semantics so a future reader doesn't misread the empty set
    as "the experience was discarded".

Both tests will fail intentionally when
`atc_durable_experience_store_writable` flips to return true for
file-backed pools, signalling the migration is complete and
this test should be updated to its post-migration form.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
